hypothesis: Enhancing SSTR2 selectivity by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 to better complement SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 interactions, and introducing charged residues at position 6 to disrupt off-target (SSTR3/5) electrostatic interactions while maintaining SSTR2 affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating strong binders but insufficiently addressing selectivity. A more focused approach on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions is needed to improve Δmargin beyond native SST-14.

proposed_changes.rationale: Target non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5 conserved-pocket contacts). Mutating these positions can disrupt off-target interactions while preserving key pharmacophore residues.

Introduce mutations at focus positions to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. These mutations are predicted to reduce off-target binding without compromising SSTR2 binding or sequence constraints.
